methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $60M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Mitra Chem is a Series B climate & energy company founded by Vivas Kumar and Will Chueh that has raised $60M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private and 1 public firm in the climate and energy sector, including later-stage peers such as KoBold Metals ($3.0B, Series C), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B, Series C), and Amogy ($700M, Series C).

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the company's $60M total raised as a proxy for current valuation and applies a typical 1.9x step-up multiplier to reflect progression from Series B to the next stage, implying a valuation of $114M. The peer-median method uses the three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables listed above; their median valuation of $1.0B, with a range of $700M to $3.0B, provides an implied valuation of $1.0B, though this reflects firms one stage further advanced.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $97M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $557M across the two methods. The substantial spread reflects uncertainty inherent in the stage-progression approach for an early-stage company and the stage mismatch in the peer set, where all comparables are Series C. Key sensitivities include the timing and success of upcoming milestones and market conditions for climate-tech capital deployment.
